L1.area: Database connectivity hooks and external service connection management

L2.description: An improper certificate validation vulnerability exists where enabling SSL/TLS for database connections causes the system to automatically disable certificate verification by default. Even when a user explicitly enables encrypted connections, server certificates are not validated, allowing potential man-in-the-middle attacks to intercept or modify traffic between the application and the database server.

L3.area: MongoDB provider connection hook and its SSL/TLS configuration handling
L3.description: An improper certificate validation vulnerability exists in the MongoDB connection layer where enabling SSL/TLS automatically sets the connection to allow invalid certificates without user consent. When a user configures ssl=true for their MongoDB connection, the system unconditionally disables certificate verification, allowing potential man-in-the-middle attacks against the MongoDB connection.

ground_truth.reason: When SSL is enabled for the MongoDB connection, the hook automatically disables certificate validation by setting tlsAllowInvalidCertificates=True, allowing man-in-the-middle attacks.
